description: US-SB-005 multi-kernel soundness validation set (real 910B3 msprof). soundness_rate must stay 1.0 (any BOUND_VIOLATION is a model bug). component_match=false where msprof attributes the whole MIX_AIC duration to aicore so a memory-bound kernel still profiles aicore-dominant (known limitation, not a soundness failure).

kernels.notes: Row-wise softmax (8192x2048 fp32). Vector-bound: aiv_scalar_ratio=0.45, aiv_mte2_ratio=0.24. HBM floor = 2*ROWS*COLS*4 / 1.525 TB/s. Correctness verified: row sums = 1.0, all values positive and finite.

LayerNorm fwd (8192x2048 fp32). Vector-scalar bound: aiv_scalar_ratio=0.52, aiv_vec_ratio=0.18. HBM floor = 2*ROWS*COLS*4 / 1.525 TB/s. Correctness verified: output finite, non-trivial variance per row.

RMSNorm fwd (8192x2048 fp32), 5th DISTINCT kernel added 2026-06-11. Mean-of-squares reduction + rsqrt scale + weight (lighter reduction than layernorm; no mean subtraction). AI_VECTOR_CORE. T_measured = median(post-warmup) = 702.86 us (11 msprof invocations). HBM floor = 2*ROWS*COLS*4 / 1.525 TB/s = 88.01 us. Correctness verified vs torch reference: max_abs_err=1.9e-6, rel=1.1e-7, finite.

us_sb_005_note: CLOSED 2026-06-11. Six entries spanning FIVE distinct kernels (chunk_kda, vector_add, softmax, layernorm, rmsnorm); rmsnorm is the new 5th distinct kernel, profiled on 910B3 with correctness verified vs torch (rel err 1.1e-7). vector_add still appears as two shape variants (16M/32M) but is no longer the only source of distinctness. soundness_rate=1.0 across all six (no BOUND_VIOLATION). Tier-2 DES coverage remains chunk_kda-only; the four vector kernels use the conservative analytic HBM floor (a sound lower bound), documented as the looser tier.

counterfactual.description: US-SB-006: work-scaling counterfactual (vector_add N=16M vs N=32M)
counterfactual.note: Valid work-scaling sanity check only; accepted US-SB-006 evidence requires a compiler-reachable seeded Gap-1/2/3/4 intervention on a semantically equivalent kernel.

two_limit.finding: Compiler headroom is negligible (0.04% of measured). Author headroom dominates at 55.8%. bishengir's lowering is near-optimal for chunk_kda.
two_limit.us_sb_008_note: Analytical-only feasibility result. chunk_kda's predicted compiler headroom (44.99us) is below measurement noise, so no equivalent hand-optimized HIVM was compiled/profiled to validate reachability. US-SB-008 requires hardware validation on a kernel with measurable compiler headroom.

scalar_calibration.description: US-SB-007: direct scalar throughput measurement from scalar_peak CCE microbench
scalar_calibration.us_sb_007_note: Measured on 910B3 with scalar_peak dependent FMA chain and explicit CANN 8.2 msprof; calib_910b3_v1.json sets vector.scalar_throughput_measured=true.
